title: Conditional Branch Router
constraints: The router must support exactly three branches: complaint, question, and feedback
Each branch must have its own handler with distinct behavior
The routing decision must be made by an LLM classification step, not keyword matching
All branches must merge back into a single output node
hints: Use a classifier node that outputs a category, then add conditional edges from that node to each branch handler
In LangGraph, use add_conditional_edges with a routing function that returns the branch name
Each branch handler should have a specialized system prompt tailored to that input type

starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: All inputs are processed the same way regardless of type
# TODO: Add conditional routing so complaints, questions, and feedback each get distinct handling
def handle_input(message: str) -> str:
    response = llm.invoke([
        SystemMessage(content="You are a customer service agent. Handle the following message."),
        HumanMessage(content=message),
    ])
    return response.content

test_inputs = [
    "Your product broke after one day! I want a refund!",
    "What payment methods do you accept?",
    "I love your new feature, the dark mode is great!",
]
for msg in test_inputs:
    result = handle_input(msg)
    print(f"Input: {msg}\nOutput: {result}\n")
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: All inputs are processed the same way regardless of type
# TODO: Add conditional routing so complaints, questions, and feedback each get distinct handling
agent = Agent(
    name="Customer Service",
    instructions="You are a customer service agent. Handle the following message.",
)

test_inputs = [
    "Your product broke after one day! I want a refund!",
    "What payment methods do you accept?",
    "I love your new feature, the dark mode is great!",
]
for msg in test_inputs:
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, msg)
    print(f"Input: {msg}\nOutput: {result.final_output}\n")
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: All inputs are processed the same way regardless of type
# TODO: Add conditional routing so complaints, questions, and feedback each get distinct handling
agent = Agent(
    role="Customer Service",
    goal="Handle customer messages",
    backstory="You are a customer service agent. Handle the following message.",
    llm=llm,
)

test_inputs = [
    "Your product broke after one day! I want a refund!",
    "What payment methods do you accept?",
    "I love your new feature, the dark mode is great!",
]

for msg in test_inputs:
    task = Task(
        description=msg,
        expected_output="A response to the customer",
        agent=agent,
    )
    crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task])
    result = crew.kickoff()
    print(f"Input: {msg}\nOutput: {result}\n")

evaluationCriteria.label: Three distinct branches
evaluationCriteria.description: The workflow contains separate handlers for complaints, questions, and feedback

evaluationCriteria.label: LLM-based routing
evaluationCriteria.description: The routing decision is made by an LLM classifier, not hardcoded keyword matching

evaluationCriteria.label: Branch-specific behavior
evaluationCriteria.description: Each branch produces noticeably different responses appropriate to the input type
